name: Swedish Lemon Angels
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Buttermilk (or 1/4 cup milk and 1/4 cup vinegar)
recipeInstructions:
PREHEAT OVEN TO 375 DEGREES F.
1. In a small bowl or measuring cup, beat the egg until foamy.
2. Add the buttermilk and vanilla and blend well.
3. Add the baking soda, one teaspoonful at a time, sprinkling it in and beating until the mixture is smooth and the consistency of light cream.
4. Add the lemon juice all at once and blend into the mixture. Stir gently; do not beat (you want it creamy, but without too much air).
5. The mixture will congeal into a pasty lump. Scoop it out of the bowl with a spatula and spread on a floured surface.
6. Sift the flour and 3/4 cup of sugar together and use your fingertips to work it into the egg-lemon mixture.
7. With a floured rolling pin, roll the dough about 1/32 inch thick. Using the tip of a sharp knife, cut out "angel" shapes and twist the edges up to form a shell-like curve about 3/8 inch high.
8. Sprinkle the remaining sugar over each shape.
9. Brush each angel with melted butter.
10. Place the angels one inch apart on an ungreased baking sheet and bake for 12 minutes, or until golden brown.
